steps.title: Count each price category
steps.content:
The first class costs $0$. The second class costs half of the regular price:
$$
\frac{22.80}{2}=11.40
$$
Since $x \ge 2$, the number of remaining regularly priced classes is $x-2$.

steps.title: Build the cost function
steps.content:
Add the half-price second class to the cost of the regularly priced classes:
$$
f(x)=22.80(x-2)+11.40
$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: The Desmos rows check the model against sample class counts. For $x=2$, $x=3$, and $x=4$, the costs are $11.40$, $34.20$, and $57.00$, matching the promotion structure. The answer is A.
